title: Staff Technical Program Manager
summary: Join us in building the future of finance. Our mission is to democratize finance for all. An estimated $124 trillion of assets will be inherited by younger generations in the next two decades. The largest transfer of wealth in human history. If you’re ready to be at the epicenter of this historic cultural and financial shift, keep reading. About the team + role We are building an elite team, applying frontier technologies to the world’s biggest financial problems. We’re looking for bold thinkers. Sharp problem-solvers. Builders who are wired to make an impact. Robinhood isn’t a place for complacency, it’s where ambitious people do the best work of their careers. We’re a high-performing, fast-moving team with ethics at the center of everything we do. Expectations are high, and so are the rewards. The Brokerage Technical Program Management team ensures our core brokerage systems remain scalable, observable, and reliable as Robinhood supports its next 10 million customers and beyond! The team partners closely with Engineering, Product, and operational partners to deliver durable platform improvements that strengthen performance, reliability, and developer efficiency. As a Technical Program Manager (IC6), you will lead complex technical programs focused on system performance, observability, and automation. You will translate platform strategy into executable plans, align senior technical stakeholders, and improve how teams plan, execute, and operate at scale. Your work will directly support the stability and growth of Robinhood’s brokerage infrastructure. This role is based in our Menlo Park, CA or New York, NY office, with in-person attendance expected at least 3 days per week. At Robinhood, we believe in the power of in-person work to accelerate progress, spark innovation, and strengthen community. Our office experience is intentional, energizing, and designed to fully support high-performing teams. What you’ll do ● Lead large-scale technical programs from early design through delivery, launch, and operational readiness across brokerage infrastructure systems ● Translate infrastructure and platform strategy into clear roadmaps, milestones, and execution plans in partnership with Product and Engineering leaders ● Identify and manage risks, dependencies, and bottlenecks related to system performance, scalability, reliability, and developer productivity ● Establish and improve lightweight program management mechanisms that increase execution speed while reducing operational overhead ● Provide consistent visibility into program status, priorities, risks, and tradeoffs for engineering, product, and executive stakeholders What you bring ● 5+ years of experience leading complex technical or engineering programs in large-scale, fast-moving environments ● Strong technical fluency, including the ability to understand system architecture, evaluate design tradeoffs, and partner closely with senior engineers ● Demonstrated ability to bring structure and measurable outcomes to ambiguous problem spaces ● Clear written and verbal communication skills, with experience influencing both technical and non-technical stakeholders ● Experience supporting or working with large-scale distributed systems Bonus points ● Background supporting financial systems, brokerage platforms, or portfolio trading systems What we offer ● Challenging, high-impact work to grow your career ● Performance driven compensation with multipliers for outsized impact, bonus programs, equity ownership, and 401(k) matching ● Best in class benefits to fuel your work, including 100% paid health insurance for employees with 90% coverage for dependents ● Lifestyle wallet – a highly flexible benefits spending account for wellness, learning, and more ● Employer-paid life & disability insurance, fertility benefits, and mental health benefits ● Time off to recharge including company holidays, paid time off, sick time, parental leave, and more! ● Exceptional office experience with cate
location: Menlo Park, CA; New York, NY

title: Corporate Development & Strategy, M&A Integration
summary: Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us! The Corporate Development & Strategy team focuses on M&A, Figma Ventures, strategic opportunities and long-term product strategy. This role is an opportunity to build a critical function at Figma at a time that our company is becoming highly acquisitive. This position will work closely with key executives and internal cross-functional team members to directly impact Figma’s future growth. This is a full time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States. What you’ll do at Figma: Drive strategic and tactical decisions and outcomes through M&A diligence, integration and beyond Captain and product manage cross-functional workstreams for all parts of M&A integration Interact directly with Figma’s executive leadership, acquired management teams, and leaders across Figma’s business Build a best-in-class integration function at Figma, enabling us to best leverage acquired assets We'd love to hear from you if you have: 10+ years of experience in business operations, corporate development, product management, consulting or other strategic business roles Ability to build and manage complex processes from start to finish and a desire to work cross-functionally Strong verbal and written communication skills Proven ability to thrive in ambiguity and build structure out of chaos While not required, it’s an added plus if you also have: Experience working within a high-growth B2B SaaS company Prior exposure to M&A At Figma, one of our values is Grow as you go. We believe in hiring smart, curious people who are excited to learn and develop their skills. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with the points outlined in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles. Pay Transparency Disclosure If based in Figma’s San Francisco or New York hub offices, this role has the annual base salary range stated below.     Job level and actual compensation will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, individual qualifications objectively assessed during the interview process (including skills and prior relevant experience, potential impact, and scope of role), market demands, and specific work location. The listed range is a guideline, and the range for this role may be modified. For roles that are available to be filled remotely, the pay range is localized according to employee work location by a factor of between 80% and 100% of range. Please discuss your specific work location with your recruiter for more information.  Figma offers equity to employees, as well a competitive package of additional benefits, including health, dental & vision, retirement with company contribution, parental leave & reproductive or family planning support, mental health & wellness benefits, generous PTO, company recharge days, a learning & development stipend, a work from home stipend, and cell phone reimbursement.  Figma also offers sales incentive pay for most sales roles and an annual bonus plan for eligible non-sales roles. Figma’s compensation and benefits are subject to change and may be modified in the future. Annual Base Salary Range: $164,000 — $288,000 USD At Figma we celebrate and support our differences. We know employing a team rich in diverse thoughts, experiences, and opinions allows our employees, our product and our community to flourish. Figma is an equa
location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States

title: Senior Manager, North America Enterprise
summary: Airbnb was born in 2007 when two hosts welcomed three guests to their San Francisco home, and has since grown to over 5 million hosts who have welcomed over 2 billion guest arrivals in almost every country across the globe. Every day, hosts offer unique stays and experiences that make it possible for guests to connect with communities in a more authentic way. The Community You Will Join:  The Homes team plays a key role in shaping the Airbnb stays experience . As a multidisciplinary team, we partner with Hosts to conceptualize and develop high-quality, repeatable, and commercially viable offerings that align with our brand values.  We are seeking an accomplished and commercially minded Senior Manager, North America Enterprise & Connectivity to lead our most critical partner relationships, spanning enterprise Property Managers and software connectivity providers. This role requires a leader with exceptional commercial acumen, executive presence, and a strong bias for action in a fast-paced, highly competitive environment. The Difference You Will Make: As Senior Manager, North America Enterprise, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of our enterprise. You will lead a team that partners with the largest Property Managers in the industry — driving growth, deepening loyalty, and delivering impact for partners, hosts, guests, and the business. You will be responsible for defining and executing the partnerships strategy, building and scaling a high-performing commercial team, and delivering measurable business impact through trusted executive relationships and innovative growth programs. A Typical Day:  Strategic & Commercial Leadership Own the Enterprise partnerships strategy for the US & Canada region. Deliver growth, retention, and expansion across high-value enterprise Property Managers. Lead negotiations and structure commercial agreements that drive mutual business outcomes. Build scalable operating playbooks and KPIs to ensure repeatable commercial success. Team & Talent Development Build and lead a high-performing commercial team with a strong execution mindset. Recruit and develop top talent, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and innovation. Establish clear career pathways and enable professional growth within the team. Partnerships & Relationship Management Cultivate deep C-suite and executive-level relationships across enterprise Property Managers. Act as a trusted advisor and be a strong thought leader. Champion the voice of enterprise partners internally to inform product, policy, and cross-functional strategies. Cross-Functional Collaboration Partner with cross-functional teams including, but not limited to,  Product, Policy, Trust, and Field to deliver integrated partner solutions and drive business results. Use data and analytics to track performance, identify opportunities, and strengthen competitive positioning. Your Expertise: 12+ years of experience in strategic partnerships, enterprise account management, or commercial leadership. Proven track record of scaling teams and exceeding ambitious commercial targets. Strong understanding of enterprise Property Managers, SaaS/software ecosystems, and connectivity platforms. Executive presence with excellent influencing, negotiation, and relationship-building skills. Analytical, data-driven, and comfortable converting insights into strategies and measurable outcomes. Thrives in a dynamic, fast-moving, and competitive environment. Your Location: This position is US - Remote Eligible. The role may include occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager. While the position is Remote Eligible, you must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity. Click here for the up-to-date list of excluded states. This list is continuously evolving, so please check back with us if the state you live in is on the exclusion list  If your position is empl

title: AI Transformation & Operations Lead
summary: Embedded directly within the Claims organization, you will serve as the bridge between operational reality and technological capability. You will work in close partnership with Technology teams to extend the impact of existing tools and identify new opportunities that require technical development or integration. You will ensure that AI systems and our claims talent work seamlessly together—delivering measurable outcomes and building a more adaptive, data-driven operating model.

title: Senior Accountant, Revenue Operations
summary: Airbnb was born in 2007 when two hosts welcomed three guests to their San Francisco home, and has since grown to over 5 million hosts who have welcomed over 2 billion guest arrivals in almost every country across the globe. Every day, hosts offer unique stays and experiences that make it possible for guests to connect with communities in a more authentic way. The Community You Will Join:  The team is responsible for accounting and financial reporting for all transactions occurring on the Airbnb and HT platforms. We ensure financial results are report accurately and timely to internal stakeholders and to external parties including governmental agencies. The Difference You Will Make: As a Senior Accountant within our Platform Accounting team, you will sit at the intersection of accounting, data, and systems to support our continued global growth. You will own end-to-end accounting activities, drive scalable and efficient processes, and strengthen our control environment. You will have broad exposure to various functions including Finance Technology, Product, Engineering, Business Development, and Customer Experience. The ideal candidate has a strong understanding of GAAP and revenue recognition, excellent technical skills, and a passion for automation and analytics in a fast-paced environment.  A Typical Day:  Manage the monthly close and financial reporting process, including preparation of routine and non-routine journal entries, reconciliations, variance analysis, and stakeholder communication. Support platform accounting impacting the income statement and balance sheet, ensuring accurate GAAP reporting and SOX compliance. Serve as a subject matter expert for accounting policies and systems, supporting new products, features, and system changes through rigorous testing and validation. Drive process improvements and ERP optimization to meet SLA/KPI targets and support scalable operations through standardization and automation. Partner cross-functionally with FP&A, Procurement, Legal, and Collections teams to align accounting outcomes with operational activity and contracts. Leverage data and automation tools such as Alteryx, SQL, and AI-enabled workflows to analyze trends, risks, and opportunities. Maintain robust documentation for accounting systems and workflows to support transitions, knowledge transfer, and cross-training initiatives. Contribute to ad hoc projects and strategic finance initiatives, effectively communicating results and updates to leadership. Your Expertise: 6+ years of relevant experience , ideally including public accounting or a high-growth tech environment. B.A. or B.S. in Accounting or Finance ; CPA certification is a strong plus. Strong knowledge of U.S. GAAP and accounting best practices, particularly regarding revenue recognition. Systems Experience: Proficiency in ERP systems (Oracle Fusion highly preferred) and experience with Alteryx or SQL is preferred. Analytical Skills: Advanced Microsoft Excel skills and experience analyzing high-volume, multi-currency transactions in a global business environment. Ownership: Ability to own projects end-to-end, building out timelines and delivering against agreed-upon specs. Communication: Ability to communicate clearly, concisely, and effectively with both finance and non-finance stakeholders. Adaptability: Capable of handling multiple projects in a fast-paced, hyper-growth environment with a positive, "can-do" approach. Compliance: Ability to define and implement internal or external controls and best practices. Your Location: This position is US - Remote Eligible. The role may include occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager. While the position is Remote Eligible, you must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity. Click here for the up-to-date list of excluded states. This list is continuously evolving, so please check back with us if the state you live in is on the e

title: AI Delivery Lead
summary: HMH’s Content Solutions Team is accelerating the integration of AI and automation across content development to improve speed, quality, consistency, and cost efficiency. The AI Delivery Lead coordinates and manages the adoption, utilization, and operational integration of AI/automation capabilities across NWEA’s products, state programs, and formative solutions. This role leads the organization and governance of AI initiatives, coordinates pilots, evaluates new capabilities, and develops SOPs/ guidelines. Additionally, this role supports the communication, training, and adoption of these initiatives across the division. The AI Delivery Lead also aligns AI/automation plans with capacity, sequencing, and agile practices to ensure predictable delivery and measurable impact.

title: Enterprise AE
summary: Our sales team is focused on finding efficient paths to successful, profitable, long-term customers by building a user-driven marketing and sales engine. To do so, we employ a bottom-up enterprise sales model by helping many small, successful customers evolve into larger, successful ones. We act as a key voice of the customer to the product team. We help identify and overcome technical, educational, and competitive obstacles to our continued growth. We are looking for an Enterprise Account Executive to join our team who is responsible for expanding our footprint within large accounts across Switzerland. You will partner closely with Customer Success, Marketing, and Product teams to identify new revenue opportunities, mitigate churn risk, and ensure our customers get the most value from Asana’s platform. This role will be instrumental in powering the Asana revenue engine through innovation & energy, consistently scaling our business and maximising value for our customers. You'll join a talented team focused on helping our current & future customers understand and leverage the power of Asana and work Management.  This role is fully remote in Switzerland. If you’re interviewing for this role, your recruiter will share more about this.  What You’ll Achieve: Proactively manage a portfolio of enterprise customers, serving as their main point of contact Hunt, manage and close new & existing business opportunities within your book of business Develop & leverage customer champions to expand usage within new teams and departments Engage with executive and IT contacts to deploy Asana wall-to-wall Champion productivity and collaboration enhancement within EMEA organizations through effective work management. Partner closely across Sales, Marketing, Engineering, and Customer Services to coordinate in-person + virtual customer interactions. Develop and execute a well-defined quarterly strategy aimed at revenue maximization for your territory. Innovate and experiment with new processes that can be replicated across Asana globally. About You: 6–10 years of sales experience, including 3–5 years in enterprise account expansion or customer growth. Proven success selling SaaS or B2B technology solutions into large enterprise clients. Experience leveraging AI-driven sales tools and modern CRM/work management platforms. Excellent collaboration, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Fluent in German and English A passion for helping customers succeed and driving impactful outcomes. Practical experience in executing and maintaining a repeatable sales process such as Sandler, MEDDIC or Challenger. Demonstrated expertise in prospecting with a track record of building your own pipeline Stellar customer service abilities, showcasing effective communication, empathy, and high integrity. End-to-end sales-cycle management skills, from prospecting to closing. Proven experience collaborating with internal marketing, business, and product teams to drive efficient, profitable, and successful customer outcomes. Proactive in setting clear business goals and priorities; able to translate strategies into actionable plans. Hold yourself and others accountable for performance goals, ensuring business consistently delivers with excellence. Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making. At Asana, we're committed to building teams that include a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills, as this is critical to helping us achieve our mission. If you're interested in this role and don't meet every listed requirement, we still encourage you to apply. What we’ll offer: Our comprehensive compensation package plays a big part in how we recognize you for the impact you have on our path to achieving our mission. We believe that compensation should be reflective of the value you create relative to the market valu
